NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. A Song. Praise is awaiting You, O God, in Zion; And to You the vow shall be performed.
NKJV.2: O You who hear prayer,  To You all flesh will come.
NKJV.3: Iniquities prevail against me; As for our transgressions, You will provide atonement for them.
NKJV.4: Blessed is the man You choose, And cause to approach You, That he may dwell in Your courts. We shall be satisfied with the goodness of Your house, Of Your holy temple.
NKJV.5: By awesome deeds in righteousness You will answer us, O God of our salvation, You who are the confidence of all the ends of the earth, And of the far-off seas;
NKJV.6: Who established the mountains by His strength, Being clothed with power;
NKJV.7: You who still the noise of the seas, The noise of their waves,  And the tumult of the peoples.
NKJV.8: They also who dwell in the farthest parts are afraid of Your signs; You make the outgoings of the morning and evening rejoice.
NKJV.9: You visit the earth and water it, You greatly enrich it;  The river of God is full of water; You provide their grain, For so You have prepared it.
NKJV.10: You water its ridges abundantly, You settle its furrows; You make it soft with showers, You bless its growth.
NKJV.11: You crown the year with Your goodness, And Your paths drip with abundance.
NKJV.12: They drop on the pastures of the wilderness, And the little hills rejoice on every side.
NKJV.13: The pastures are clothed with flocks;  The valleys also are covered with grain; They shout for joy, they also sing.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> and Song<S>7892</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Praise<S>8416</S> waiteth<S>1747</S> for thee, O God<S>430</S>, in Sion<S>6726</S>: and unto thee shall the vow<S>5088</S> be performed<S>7999</S>. <sup>waiteth: Heb. is silent</sup>
KJV.2: O thou that hearest<S>8085</S> prayer<S>8605</S>, unto thee shall all flesh<S>1320</S> come<S>935</S>.
KJV.3: Iniquities<S>1697</S> <S>5771</S> prevail<S>1396</S> against me: as for our transgressions<S>6588</S>, thou shalt purge them away<S>3722</S>. <sup>iniquities: Heb. Words, or, Matters of iniquities</sup>
KJV.4: Blessed<S>835</S> is the man whom thou choosest<S>977</S>, and causest to approach<S>7126</S> unto thee, that he may dwell<S>7931</S> in thy courts<S>2691</S>: we shall be satisfied<S>7646</S> with the goodness<S>2898</S> of thy house<S>1004</S>, even of thy holy<S>6918</S> temple<S>1964</S>.
KJV.5: By terrible things<S>3372</S> in righteousness<S>6664</S> wilt thou answer<S>6030</S> us, O God<S>430</S> of our salvation<S>3468</S>; who art the confidence<S>4009</S> of all the ends<S>7099</S> of the earth<S>776</S>, and of them that are afar off<S>7350</S> upon the sea<S>3220</S>:
KJV.6: Which by his strength<S>3581</S> setteth fast<S>3559</S> the mountains<S>2022</S>; being girded<S>247</S> with power<S>1369</S>:
KJV.7: Which stilleth<S>7623</S> the noise<S>7588</S> of the seas<S>3220</S>, the noise<S>7588</S> of their waves<S>1530</S>, and the tumult<S>1995</S> of the people<S>3816</S>.
KJV.8: They also that dwell<S>3427</S> in the uttermost parts<S>7099</S> are afraid<S>3372</S> at thy tokens<S>226</S>: thou makest the outgoings<S>4161</S> of the morning<S>1242</S> and evening<S>6153</S> to rejoice<S>7442</S>. <sup>rejoice: or, sing</sup>
KJV.9: Thou visitest<S>6485</S> the earth<S>776</S>, and waterest<S>7783</S> it: thou greatly<S>7227</S> enrichest<S>6238</S> it with the river<S>6388</S> of God<S>430</S>, which is full<S>4390</S> of water<S>4325</S>: thou preparest<S>3559</S> them corn<S>1715</S>, when thou hast so provided<S>3559</S> for it. <sup>and: or, after thou hadst made it to desire rain</sup>
KJV.10: Thou waterest<S>7301</S> the ridges<S>8525</S> thereof abundantly<S>7301</S>: thou settlest<S>5181</S> the furrows<S>1417</S> <S>1418</S> thereof: thou makest it soft<S>4127</S> with showers<S>7241</S>: thou blessest<S>1288</S> the springing<S>6780</S> thereof. <sup>settlest: or, causest rain to descend into</sup> <sup>makest: Heb. dissolvest it</sup>
KJV.11: Thou crownest<S>5849</S> the year<S>8141</S> with thy goodness<S>2896</S>; and thy paths<S>4570</S> drop<S>7491</S> fatness<S>1880</S>. <sup>with: Heb. of</sup>
KJV.12: They drop<S>7491</S> upon the pastures<S>4999</S> of the wilderness<S>4057</S>: and the little hills<S>1389</S> rejoice<S>1524</S> on every side<S>2296</S>. <sup>rejoice: Heb. are girded with joy</sup>
KJV.13: The pastures<S>3733</S> are clothed<S>3847</S> with flocks<S>6629</S>; the valleys<S>6010</S> also are covered over<S>5848</S> with corn<S>1250</S>; they shout for joy<S>7321</S>, they also sing<S>7891</S>.

ESV.1: Praise is due to you,  O God, in Zion, and to you shall vows be performed.
ESV.2: O you who hear prayer, to you shall all flesh come.
ESV.3: When iniquities prevail against me, you atone for our transgressions.
ESV.4: Blessed is the one you choose and bring near, to dwell in your courts! We shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house, the holiness of your temple!
ESV.5: By awesome deeds you answer us with righteousness, O God of our salvation, the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest seas;
ESV.6: the one who by his strength established the mountains, being girded with might;
ESV.7: who stills the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, the tumult of the peoples,
ESV.8: so that those who dwell at the ends of the earth are in awe at your signs. You make the going out of the morning and the evening to shout for joy.
ESV.9: You visit the earth and water it;  you greatly enrich it; the river of God is full of water; you provide their grain, for so you have prepared it.
ESV.10: You water its furrows abundantly, settling its ridges, softening it with showers, and blessing its growth.
ESV.11: You crown the year with your bounty; your wagon tracks overflow with abundance.
ESV.12: The pastures of the wilderness overflow, the hills gird themselves with joy,
ESV.13: the meadows clothe themselves with flocks, the valleys deck themselves with grain, they shout and sing together for joy.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. A Song. There will be silence before You, [and] praise in Zion, O God, And to You the vow will be performed.
NASB.2: O You who hear prayer, To You all men come.
NASB.3: Iniquities prevail against me; As for our transgressions, You forgive them.
NASB.4: How blessed is the one whom You choose and bring near [to You] To dwell in Your courts. We will be satisfied with the goodness of Your house, Your holy temple.
NASB.5: By awesome [deeds] You answer us in righteousness, O God of our salvation, You who are the trust of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest sea;
NASB.6: Who establishes the mountains by His strength, Being girded with might;
NASB.7: Who stills the roaring of the seas, The roaring of their waves, And the tumult of the peoples.
NASB.8: They who dwell in the ends [of the earth] stand in awe of Your signs; You make the dawn and the sunset shout for joy.
NASB.9: You visit the earth and cause it to overflow; You greatly enrich it; The stream of God is full of water; You prepare their grain, for thus You prepare the earth.
NASB.10: You water its furrows abundantly, You settle its ridges, You soften it with showers, You bless its growth.
NASB.11: You have crowned the year with Your bounty, And Your paths drip [with] fatness.
NASB.12: The pastures of the wilderness drip, And the hills gird themselves with rejoicing.
NASB.13: The meadows are clothed with flocks And the valleys are covered with grain; They shout for joy, yes, they sing.

NLT.1:
What mighty praise, O God,
belongs to you in Zion.
We will fulfill our vows to you,
NLT.2:
for you answer our prayers.
All of us must come to you.
NLT.3:
Though we are overwhelmed by our sins,
you forgive them all.
NLT.4:
What joy for those you choose to bring near,
those who live in your holy courts.
What festivities await us
inside your holy Temple.
NLT.5:
You faithfully answer our prayers with awesome deeds,
O God our savior.
You are the hope of everyone on earth,
even those who sail on distant seas.
NLT.6:
You formed the mountains by your power
and armed yourself with mighty strength.
NLT.7:
You quieted the raging oceans
with their pounding waves
and silenced the shouting of the nations.
NLT.8:
Those who live at the ends of the earth
stand in awe of your wonders.
From where the sun rises to where it sets,
you inspire shouts of joy.
NLT.9:
You take care of the earth and water it,
making it rich and fertile.
The river of God has plenty of water;
it provides a bountiful harvest of grain,
for you have ordered it so.
NLT.10:
You drench the plowed ground with rain,
melting the clods and leveling the ridges.
You soften the earth with showers
and bless its abundant crops.
NLT.11:
You crown the year with a bountiful harvest;
even the hard pathways overflow with abundance.
NLT.12:
The grasslands of the wilderness become a lush pasture,
and the hillsides blossom with joy.
NLT.13:
The meadows are clothed with flocks of sheep,
and the valleys are carpeted with grain.
They all shout and sing for joy!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. A song. Praise waits for you, God, in Zion. To you shall vows be performed.
WEB.2: You who hear prayer, to you all men will come.
WEB.3: Sins overwhelmed me, but you atoned for our transgressions.
WEB.4: Blessed is one whom you choose, and cause to come near, that he may live in your courts. We will be filled with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.
WEB.5: By awesome deeds of righteousness, you answer us, God of our salvation. You who are the hope of all the ends of the earth, of those who are far away on the sea;
WEB.6: Who by his power forms the mountains, having armed yourself with strength;
WEB.7: who stills the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the turmoil of the nations.
WEB.8: They also who dwell in faraway places are afraid at your wonders. You call the morning’s dawn and the evening with songs of joy.
WEB.9: You visit the earth, and water it. You greatly enrich it. The river of God is full of water. You provide them grain, for so you have ordained it.
WEB.10: You drench its furrows. You level its ridges. You soften it with showers. You bless it with a crop.
WEB.11: You crown the year with your bounty. Your carts overflow with abundance.
WEB.12: The wilderness grasslands overflow. The hills are clothed with gladness.
WEB.13: The pastures are covered with flocks. The valleys also are clothed with grain. They shout for joy! They also sing.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. A song. [1] Praise is rightfully yours, [1]
God, in Zion;
vows to you will be fulfilled. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
All humanity will come to you,
the one who hears prayer. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
Iniquities overwhelm me;
only you can atone for our rebellions. ⓚ
CSB17.4:
How happy is the one you choose
and bring near to live in your courts!
We will be satisfied with the goodness of your house,
the holiness of your temple. [2] ⓛ
CSB17.5:
You answer us in righteousness,
with awe-inspiring works,
God of our salvation,
the hope of all the ends of the earth
and of the distant seas. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
You establish the mountains by your power;
you are robed with strength. ⓝ
CSB17.7:
You silence the roar of the seas,
the roar of their waves,
and the tumult of the nations. ⓐ
CSB17.8:
Those who live far away are awed by your signs;
you make east and west shout for joy. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
You visit the earth and water it abundantly,
enriching it greatly.
God’s stream is filled with water,
for you prepare the earth in this way,
providing people with grain. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
You soften it with showers and bless its growth,
soaking its furrows and leveling its ridges. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
You crown the year with your goodness;
your carts overflow with plenty. [3] ⓔ
CSB17.12:
The wilderness pastures overflow,
and the hills are robed with joy. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
The pastures are clothed with flocks
and the valleys covered with grain. ⓖ
They shout in triumph; indeed, they sing. ⓗ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. A Song. To You belongs silence [the submissive wonder of reverence], and [it bursts into] praise in Zion, O God;
And to You the vow shall be performed.
AMP.2:
O You who hear prayer,
To You all mankind comes.
AMP.3:
Wickedness and guilt prevail against me;
Yet as for our transgressions,
You forgive them [removing them from Your sight].
AMP.4:
Blessed is the one whom You choose and bring near
To dwell in Your courts.
We will be filled with the goodness of Your house,
Your holy temple.
AMP.5:
By awesome and wondrous things You answer us in righteousness, O God of our salvation,
You who are the trust and hope of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest sea;
AMP.6:
Who creates the mountains by His strength,
Being clothed with power,
AMP.7:
Who stills the roaring of the seas,
The roaring of their waves,
And the tumult of the peoples,
AMP.8:
So they who dwell at the ends of the earth stand in awe of Your signs [the evidence of Your presence].
You make the dawn and the sunset shout for joy.
AMP.9:
You visit the earth and make it overflow [with water];
You greatly enrich it;
The stream of God is full of water;
You provide their grain, when You have prepared the earth.
AMP.10:
You water its furrows abundantly,
You smooth its ridges;
You soften it with showers,
You bless its growth.
AMP.11:
You crown the year with Your bounty,
And Your paths overflow.
AMP.12:
The pastures of the wilderness drip [with dew],
And the hills are encircled with joy.
AMP.13:
The meadows are clothed with flocks
And the valleys are covered with grain;
They shout for joy and they sing.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Silence is praise to you,
Zion-dwelling God,
And also obedience.
MSG.2:
You hear the prayer in it all.
We all arrive at your doorstep sooner
or later, loaded with guilt,
MSG.3:
Our sins too much for us—
but you get rid of them once and for all.
MSG.4:
Blessed are the chosen! Blessed the guest
at home in your place!
We expect our fill of good things
in your house, your heavenly manse.
MSG.5:
All your salvation wonders
are on display in your trophy room.
Earth-Tamer, Ocean-Pourer,
MSG.7:
Muzzler of sea storm and wave crash,
of mobs in noisy riot—
MSG.8:
Far and wide they'll come to a stop,
they'll stare in awe, in wonder.
Dawn and dusk take turns
calling, "Come and worship."
MSG.9:
Oh, visit the earth,
ask her to join the dance!
Deck her out in spring showers,
fill the God-River with living water.
Paint the wheat fields golden.
Creation was made for this!
MSG.10:
Drench the plowed fields,
soak the dirt clods
With rainfall as harrow and rake
bring her to blossom and fruit.
MSG.11:
Snow-crown the peaks with splendor,
scatter rose petals down your paths,
MSG.12:
All through the wild meadows, rose petals.
Set the hills to dancing,
MSG.13:
Dress the canyon walls with live sheep,
a drape of flax across the valleys.
Let them shout, and shout, and shout!
Oh, oh, let them sing!

NIV.1:
Psalm 65
For the director of music. A psalm of David. A song.
Praise awaits you, O God, in Zion;
to you our vows will be fulfilled.
NIV.2:
O you who hear prayer,
to you all men will come.
NIV.3:
When we were overwhelmed by sins,
you forgave our transgressions.
NIV.4:
Blessed are those you choose
and bring near to live in your courts!
We are filled with the good things of your house,
of your holy temple.
NIV.5:
You answer us with awesome deeds of righteousness,
O God our Savior,
the hope of all the ends of the earth
and of the farthest seas,
NIV.6:
who formed the mountains by your power,
having armed yourself with strength,
NIV.7:
who stilled the roaring of the seas,
the roaring of their waves,
and the turmoil of the nations.
NIV.8:
Those living far away fear your wonders;
where morning dawns and evening fades
you call forth songs of joy.
NIV.9:
You care for the land and water it;
you enrich it abundantly.
The streams of God are filled with water
to provide the people with grain,
for so you have ordained it.
NIV.10:
You drench its furrows
and level its ridges;
you soften it with showers
and bless its crops.
NIV.11:
You crown the year with your bounty,
and your carts overflow with abundance.
NIV.12:
The grasslands of the desert overflow;
the hills are clothed with gladness.
NIV.13:
The meadows are covered with flocks
and the valleys are mantled with grain;
they shout for joy and sing.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise is due to you,
O God, in Zion;
and to you shall vows be performed,
NRSVCE.2:
O you who answer prayer!
To you all flesh shall come.
NRSVCE.3:
When deeds of iniquity overwhelm us,
you forgive our transgressions.
NRSVCE.4:
Happy are those whom you choose and bring near
to live in your courts.
We shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house,
your holy temple.
NRSVCE.5:
By awesome deeds you answer us with deliverance,
O God of our salvation;
you are the hope of all the ends of the earth
and of the farthest seas.
NRSVCE.6:
By your strength you established the mountains;
you are girded with might.
NRSVCE.7:
You silence the roaring of the seas,
the roaring of their waves,
the tumult of the peoples.
NRSVCE.8:
Those who live at earth’s farthest bounds are awed by your signs;
you make the gateways of the morning and the evening shout for joy.
NRSVCE.9:
You visit the earth and water it,
you greatly enrich it;
the river of God is full of water;
you provide the people with grain,
for so you have prepared it.
NRSVCE.10:
You water its furrows abundantly,
settling its ridges,
softening it with showers,
and blessing its growth.
NRSVCE.11:
You crown the year with your bounty;
your wagon tracks overflow with richness.
NRSVCE.12:
The pastures of the wilderness overflow,
the hills gird themselves with joy,
NRSVCE.13:
the meadows clothe themselves with flocks,
the valleys deck themselves with grain,
they shout and sing together for joy.

NET.1: Praise awaits you, O God, in Zion. Vows made to you are fulfilled.
NET.2: You hear prayers; all people approach you.
NET.3: Our record of sins overwhelms me, but you forgive our acts of rebellion.
NET.4: How blessed is the one whom you choose, and allow to live in your palace courts. May we be satisfied with the good things of your house — your holy palace.
NET.5: You answer our prayers by performing awesome acts of deliverance, O God, our savior. All the ends of the earth trust in you, as well as those living across the wide seas.
NET.6: You created the mountains by your power, and demonstrated your strength.
NET.7: You calm the raging seas and their roaring waves, as well as the commotion made by the nations.
NET.8: Even those living in the most remote areas are awestruck by your acts; you cause those living in the east and west to praise you.
NET.9: You visit the earth and give it rain; you make it rich and fertile with overflowing streams full of water. You provide grain for them, for you prepare the earth to yield its crops.
NET.10: You saturate its furrows, and soak its plowed ground. With rain showers you soften its soil, and make its crops grow.
NET.11: You crown the year with your good blessings, and you leave abundance in your wake.
NET.12: The pastures in the wilderness glisten with moisture, and the hills are clothed with joy.
NET.13: The meadows are clothed with sheep, and the valleys are covered with grain. They shout joyfully, yes, they sing.
